symptoms.symptom_name: Gross deformity with complete inability to move the shoulder

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic nerve distribution affected in anterior shoulder dislocation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal hand colour, sensation, and movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring for distal vascular supply, though ongoing monitoring is still needed.
First-time dislocation, relevant to long-term management planning.

symptoms.symptom_name: Empty glenoid joint space, squared-off shoulder contour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Gross deformity with complete loss of movement and axillary nerve symptoms together confirm dislocation with nerve involvement requiring urgent in-person reduction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent Emergency Department X-ray followed by reduction under procedural sedation/analgesia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms absence of associated fracture before safe reduction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Post-reduction neurovascular reassessment and repeat X-ray
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms successful relocation and resolution/persistence of nerve findings.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Attempting reduction at home without imaging
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Risks fracture, worsened nerve injury, or vascular damage without proper technique and prior imaging.
